source: Utah State Construction Code, IRC/IBC as adopted, local jurisdiction amendments

state_code.base_code: 2021 International Residential Code (IRC) with Utah amendments, transitioning to 2024 IBC/IRC per HB65
state_code.fire_code: 2021 International Fire Code (IFC) with Utah amendments
state_code.mechanical_code: 2021 International Mechanical Code (IMC) with Utah amendments
key_utah_amendments: R301.2.3 Snow loads: Where ground snow loads exceed 75 psf, eaves must sustain 2x psf on overhangs
R301.6 Utah Snow Loads: Ground snow loads per local jurisdiction or ASCE 7 Figure 7.2-1
R313.1 Sprinkler systems: Not required for one- and two-family dwellings unless local jurisdiction requires
R602.10.1 Braced wall panels: Continuous structural panel sheathing method allowed as alternative
R802.10.4 Truss design: Trusses must be designed by registered engineer, sealed drawings required
Energy code: Prescriptive R-values for Climate Zone 5 (most of Utah) and Zone 6 (mountain areas)

summit_county.name: Summit County
special_requirements: Wildland-Urban Interface (WUI) requirements may apply in mountain areas
Steep slope building may require geotechnical report
Snow country design: roof snow loads often exceed 100 psf, drift loads must be calculated
Engineered foundation required for most custom homes
Radon mitigation system required in new construction
Fire sprinklers may be required based on access and fire district
Defensible space requirements in WUI zones
permit_requirements: Building permit application with 2 sets of plans
Engineered plans required for custom homes
SWPPP (Stormwater Pollution Prevention Plan) for sites > 1 acre
Septic permit if not on sewer
Access permit from county road department

park_city.name: Park City
special_requirements: Historic district overlay may apply — check before design
Height restrictions vary by zone (28-35 ft typical)
Snow storage requirements on site
Heated driveway/walkway requirements in some zones
Green building incentives available

powder_mountain.name: Powder Mountain / Eden / Ogden Valley
special_requirements: Extreme snow loads — 150+ psf ground snow load at higher elevations
Roof design must account for unbalanced snow loads and drift
Metal roofing with snow guards common/recommended
Structural engineer required for all custom homes
Access road grades limited — may need heated driveways
Fire district requirements for sprinklers based on response time
Avalanche hazard zones — check before building
Well and septic common — percolation test required

morgan_county.name: Morgan County / Morgan City
special_requirements: IRC adopted with state amendments
One- and two-family dwellings per IRC Chapter 11 (energy)
Automatic fire sprinklers per IRC R313 as amended by state
Soils report may be required for expansive soils
Flood zone check required — FEMA maps
Agricultural exemptions may apply for outbuildings

layton.name: Layton City
special_requirements: Standard IRC/IBC with state amendments
Residential plan review typically 2-3 weeks
Impact fees apply for new construction
Landscaping requirements for new homes
Driveway approach permit from public works

salt_lake_city.name: Salt Lake City
special_requirements: Historic preservation overlay in many neighborhoods
ADU (Accessory Dwelling Unit) regulations — check zoning
Solar-ready requirements for new construction
EV charging infrastructure requirements
Stormwater management requirements
Tree protection ordinance during construction

coalville.name: Coalville City
special_requirements: Small city — uses Summit County building department for inspections
Zoning check required before permit application
Water and sewer connection fees
Moderate snow loads — engineered trusses recommended

wall_framing.studs: 2x4 or 2x6 at 16 in OC (24 in OC allowed for some conditions)
wall_framing.top_plates: Double top plate required, single allowed with approved connectors
wall_framing.bottom_plate: Pressure-treated on concrete, standard on wood subfloor
wall_framing.bracing: Per IRC R602.10 — continuous sheathing or let-in bracing
wall_framing.fire_blocking: Required at 10ft vertical intervals, at floor/ceiling levels, at soffits, and at connections between horizontal and vertical spaces

floor_framing.joists: Per IRC span tables — species, grade, spacing, and load determine size
floor_framing.bridging: Required at 8ft intervals for joists deeper than 2x12
floor_framing.subfloor: 3/4 in T&G plywood or OSB, glued and nailed
floor_framing.cantilever: Maximum 1/4 of backspan for floor joists

roof_framing.rafters: Per IRC span tables or engineered
roof_framing.collar_ties: Required in upper 1/3 of attic space, 4ft OC maximum
roof_framing.ridge_board: Minimum 1x, depth equal to cut end of rafter
roof_framing.ridge_beam: Required for cathedral ceilings without collar ties — must be engineered
roof_framing.sheathing: 7/16 in OSB or 1/2 in plywood minimum, H-clips at unsupported edges

fastener_schedule.sheathing_to_framing_walls: 8d common at 6 in OC edges, 12 in OC field
fastener_schedule.sheathing_to_framing_roof: 8d common at 6 in OC edges, 12 in OC field
fastener_schedule.subfloor_to_joist: 8d common at 6 in OC edges, 12 in OC field (plus construction adhesive)

inspection_sequence: 1. Footing inspection — before concrete pour, verify rebar, depth, width
2. Foundation inspection — forms, rebar, anchor bolts before pour
4. Slab inspection — vapor barrier, rebar, thickness
5. Framing inspection — ALL framing complete, sheathing, nailing, connectors, fire blocking
6. Rough plumbing — supply and DWV before cover
7. Rough electrical — wiring, boxes, panels before cover
8. Rough mechanical — ductwork, equipment before cover
9. Insulation inspection — R-values, vapor barrier, air sealing
11. Final inspections — plumbing, electrical, mechanical, building
common_code_violations_framing: Missing or incorrect hurricane ties/straps at roof-to-wall connection
Missing fire blocking at floor levels and soffits
Incorrect nailing schedule — too few nails or wrong size
Missing holdowns at shear wall ends
Studs not aligned with joists/trusses above (stacking)
Missing cripple studs below window sills
Notching or boring joists/studs beyond code limits
Incorrect Simpson connector installation — missing nails, wrong nail type
